title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement joshua.marie.k@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Getting Started
text: tabstats lightweight package displaying data clean, formatted tables directly console. mainly designed developers wants display outputs (preferably data frame object), make output readable glance, configurable without boilerplate, friendly interactive workflows. package provides four main functions: functions share common design philosophy: Styling controlled via style = list(...) argument accepts either predefined style strings (e.g. "blue_bold") plain lambda functions (e.g. \(x) cli::col_red(x)). Alignment controlled via align argument accepts single string, length-2 vector, named list. Centering controlled via center_table = TRUE, automatically pads output middle terminal.

title: Getting Started
text: Install following package CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("tabstats") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("joshuamarie/tabstats") ## devtools::install_github("joshuamarie/tabstats")

title: Getting Started
text: main API package displays data wanted display specific format

title: Getting Started
text: Data frames usually looks like displayed: table_default(), data frame now displayed APA-style format, configurable formatting.

title: Getting Started
text: Another form table_default(), except best suitable displaying summarized data, requires 2 columns data frame. simplest use case named summary values, like model diagnostics descriptive statistics, 1st column name parameter (statistic) 2nd column estimate. header means still wanted display column names original df data frame, otherwise, column names present.
code: df = data.frame(     Statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),     Value = c("100", "3.45", "1.20", "1.00", "6.00") )  table_summary(     df,      title = "Descriptive Statistics",      header = TRUE ) Descriptive Statistics ----------------------   Statistic    Value ----------------------   N              100   Mean          3.45   SD            1.20   Min           1.00   Max           6.00 ----------------------

title: Getting Started
text: much complex niche function display matrix, specifically designed correlation matrices. display correlation matrix, typically output cor(), can directly pass . , data wanted display matrix, another form, ’re going configure building custom spec new_corr_data() full control values appear. wild example, assuming want display output rstatix::cor_test():

title: Getting Started
text: Another nice function display matrices — contingency tables, exact. (almost) everything display contingency table, including observed frequencies, expected values, percentages side side.

title: Getting Started
text: table functions provides API style table. table_default() 2 parameters: style_colnames style column names wanted design, style_columns want apply style entire column choose style. Use td_style() configure style column choose style. table_summary() style parameter configure style specific column wanted design. Use sm_style() configure style specific column. cross_table() style parameter configure style data displayed function. Use ct_style() configure style displayed values. corr_matrix() style parameter configure style values entered, typically display argument using new_corr_data(). Use cm_style() configure values assigned new_corr_data() displayed table. quickest way style output named string: full control, pass lambda instead — receives text argument: Supported named style strings: example: Imagine want apply p-value output rstatix::cor_test(), earlier example. corr_matrix(), can even conditionally format specified value new_corr_data().
code: table_summary(     df,      title = "Descriptive Statistics",      header = TRUE,      style = sm_style(         left_col = "blue_bold",         right_col = "green",         title = "bold",          sep = ":  "     ) ) Descriptive Statistics  -----------------------   Statistic    Value -----------------------   N         :     100   Mean      :    3.45   SD        :    1.20   Min       :    1.00   Max       :    6.00 ----------------------- table_summary(     df,      title = "Descriptive Statistics",      header = TRUE,      style = sm_style(         left_col = \(x) cli::col_red(cli::style_bold(x)),         right_col = \(x) cli::col_cyan(x),          title = "bold",          sep = ":  "     ) ) Descriptive Statistics  -----------------------   Statistic    Value -----------------------   N         :     100   Mean      :    3.45   SD        :    1.20   Min       :    1.00   Max       :    6.00 ----------------------- cor_mat |>      with({         corr_matrix(             new_corr_data(                 var1 = var1,                 var2 = var2,                 corr = cor,                 statistic = statistic,                 pval = p,                 conf_int = conf_int             ),             title = "Pearson Correlation Matrix",             style = cm_style(                 pval = function(x) {                     x_num = as.numeric(x)                     if (is.na(x_num) || x_num > 0.05) {                         cli::style_italic(x)                      } else if (x_num > 0.01) {                         cli::col_red(x)                     } else {                         cli::style_bold("<0.001")                     }                 }             )         )     }) Pearson Correlation Matrix                       ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   Variable         Sepal.Width      Sepal.Length      Petal.Length     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   Sepal.Width           1               -0.12             -0.43                                              -1.4e+00          -5.8e+00                                               0.152            <0.001                                           [-0.27,  0.044]   [-0.55, -0.288]   ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   Sepal.Length        -0.12               1                0.87                            -1.4e+00                             2.2e+01                             0.152                              <0.001                         [-0.27,  0.044]                     [ 0.83,  0.906]   ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────   Petal.Length        -0.43              0.87               1                              -5.8e+00           2.2e+01                                              <0.001            <0.001                                           [-0.55, -0.288]   [ 0.83,  0.906]                     ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

what: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix
title: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix
text: Display Correlation Matrix Table Console

title: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix
code: corr_matrix(   display,   title = NULL,   diag_1 = TRUE,   digits = 3,   layout_view = FALSE,   layout_center = FALSE,   center_table = FALSE,   border_char = getOption("tab_default")$border_char,   style = list(),   ... )

title: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix
text: display corr_spec object new_corr_data(), plain symmetric matrix (e.g. cor()). title Label shown title (e.g. "Pearson Correlation Matrix"). Auto-detected title attribute spec present. diag_1 TRUE, diagonal cells always show "1". Default TRUE. digits Decimal places numeric formatting. Default 3. layout_view Show layout legend box table? Default FALSE. layout_center Center layout box terminal? Default FALSE. center_table Center table terminal? Default FALSE. border_char Border character. Default getOption("tab_default"). style cm_style() object. Keys match extra field names passed new_corr_data() (e.g. rho, pval, bf), plus title border_text. ... Reserved future use.

title: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix
text: Invisibly returns rendered character matrix.

title: Display a Correlation Matrix Table in the Console — corr_matrix

text: data matrix table observed frequencies. percentage percentages show. Options: TRUE/"", "by_row", "by_col", "by_total", FALSE/NULL. layout Show layout legend box? Default TRUE. expected Show expected frequencies? Default TRUE. layout_center Center layout box? Default FALSE. header_underline Shorten underline beneath column header? Default FALSE. digits Named list keys ex, row_pct, col_pct, total_pct. Falls back getOption("ct_digits"). center_table Center entire table terminal? Default FALSE. style Named list supplied using ct_style(). ... Reserved future use.

what: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default
title: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default
text: Display formatted table console

title: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default
code: table_default(   x,   justify_cols = "center",   digits = 3,   digits_by_col = NULL,   scientific = FALSE,   na_print = "",   min_width = NULL,   border_char = options("tab_default")$tab_default$border_char,   show_row_names = FALSE,   center_table = FALSE,   n_space = 2,   title = NULL,   style_colnames = NULL,   style_columns = NULL,   nrows = getOption("tab_default")$nrows,   vb = list(),   auto_wrap = TRUE,   wrap_threshold = 1,   ... )

title: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default
text: x data frame tibble. justify_cols Alignment: single string, vector, named list "left"/"right"/"center". digits Digits round numeric columns . Default 3. digits_by_col Named list per-column digit overrides. scientific Display numerics scientific notation? Default FALSE. na_print String missing values. Default "". min_width Minimum column width. Default NULL. border_char Character borders. Default "\u2500". show_row_names Show row names? Default FALSE. center_table Center table terminal? Default FALSE. n_space Spaces columns. Default 2. title Optional title string table. td_style(), named list name column name "title", value either cli style string (e.g. "blue_bold") function \(ctx) ... receiving context list. style_colnames Styling column header cells. td_style object td_style(), named list name column name "title", value either cli style string (e.g. "blue_bold") function \(ctx) ... receiving context list. style_columns Styling data cells. td_style object td_style(), named list name column name column index string, value cli style string function \(ctx) ... receiving context list elements value, formatted_value, col_name, col_index, is_header, data, justify, width. nrows Max rows display truncation. vb Vertical border spec: list(char = "\u2502", = c(1, 3)). auto_wrap Auto-wrap wide tables? Default TRUE. wrap_threshold Fraction console width wrapping. Default 1. ... Reserved future use.

title: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default
text: Invisibly returns input data character matrix formatting applied. function called primarily side effect printing styled table R console. Returns invisible(NULL) early input 0 rows 0 columns, 0 columns.

title: Display a formatted table in the console — table_default

what: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
title: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
text: function takes two-column data frame formats summary-like table. table can optionally split two parts, centered, given title. useful displaying summary information clean, tabular format. function also supports styling ANSI colors text formatting {cli} package column alignment options.

title: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
code: table_summary(   data,   title = NULL,   l = NULL,   header = FALSE,   center_table = FALSE,   border_char = "-",   style = list(),   align = NULL,   ... )

title: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
text: data data frame exactly two columns. data summarized displayed. title character string. optional title displayed table. l integer. number rows include left part split table. NULL, table split. header logical value. TRUE, column names data displayed header. center_table logical value. TRUE, table centered terminal. border_char Character used borders. Default "\u2500". style list controlling visual styling table elements using ANSI formatting. Can include following components: left_col: Styling left column values. right_col: Styling right column values. border_text: Styling border. title: Styling title. sep: Separator character left right column. style component can either predefined style string (e.g., "blue", "red_italic", "bold") function takes context list /without value element returns styled text. align Controls alignment column values. Can specified three ways: single string: affects left column (e.g., "left", "center", "right"). vector two strings: affects columns order (e.g., c("left", "right")). list named components: explicitly specifies alignment column ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
text: function return value. prints formatted table console.

title: Summarize and Display a Two-Column Data Frame as a Formatted Table — table_summary
code: # Create a sample data frame df = data.frame(     Category = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"),     Value = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50) )  # Display the table with a title and header table_summary(df, title = "Sample Table", header = TRUE) #>  #>     Sample Table      #> --------------------- #>   Category    Value #> --------------------- #>   A              10 #>   B              20 #>   C              30 #>   D              40 #>   E              50 #> ---------------------  # Split the table after the second row and center it table_summary(df, l = 2, center_table = TRUE) #> Warning: running command 'tput cols' had status 2 #> ------------------------------------------ #>   A              10    C              30 #>   B              20    D              40 #>                        E              50 #> ------------------------------------------  # Use styling and alignment table_summary(     df,     header = TRUE,     style = list(         left_col = "blue_bold",         right_col = "red",         title = "green",         border_text = "yellow"     ),     align = c("center", "right") ) #> --------------------- #>   Category    Value #> --------------------- #>      A           10 #>      B           20 #>      C           30 #>      D           40 #>      E           50 #> ---------------------  # Use custom styling with lambda functions table_summary(     df,     header = TRUE,     style = sm_style(         left_col = \(ctx) cli::col_red(ctx), # ctx$value is another option         right_col = \(ctx) cli::col_blue(ctx)     ),     align = list(left_col = "left", right_col = "right") ) #> --------------------- #>   Category    Value #> --------------------- #>   A              10 #>   B              20 #>   C              30 #>   D              40 #>   E              50 #> ---------------------

what: tabstats: A Lightweight Toolkit for Displaying Customizable Tables — tabstats
title: tabstats: A Lightweight Toolkit for Displaying Customizable Tables — tabstats
text: lightweight toolkit provides functions printing tables input data R console terminal customizable formatting. Supported outputs include American Psychological Association (APA)-style tables (American Psychological Association, 2020, ISBN:9781433832178), correlation matrices, contingency tables, two-column summary tables.

title: tabstats: A Lightweight Toolkit for Displaying Customizable Tables — tabstats
text: Maintainer: Joshua Marie joshua.marie.k@gmail.com

title: Manage package options — tabstats_options
text: function allows retrieving modifying package options across different categories. called without arguments, returns option categories values. category provided alone, returns options category. category option provided, returns specific option value. three parameters provided, updates specified option.

title: Manage package options — tabstats_options
code: tabstats_options(category = NULL, option = NULL, value = NULL)

title: Manage package options — tabstats_options
text: category character string specifying option category (e.g., "tab_default", "tab_digits"). omitted, returns option categories. option character string specifying option retrieve modify within category. backward compatibility, can also use specific option name directly category parameter. value new value assign specified option. NULL, function returns current value.

title: Manage package options — tabstats_options
text: arguments provided, returns option categories values. category provided, returns options category. category option provided without value, returns current value option. parameters provided, updates option returns updated option category list invisibly.

title: Manage package options — tabstats_options
code: # Get all options across all categories tabstats_options() #> $tab_default #> $tab_default$vb_char #> [1] "│" #>  #> $tab_default$vb_top #> [1] "┬" #>  #> $tab_default$vb_mid #> [1] "┼" #>  #> $tab_default$vb_bottom #> [1] "┴" #>  #> $tab_default$border_char #> [1] "─" #>  #> $tab_default$header_underline #> [1] "─" #>  #> $tab_default$truncate_message #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $tab_default$nrows #> [1] 10 #>  #>  #> $tab_digits #> $tab_digits$ex #> [1] 1 #>  #> $tab_digits$row_pct #> [1] 0 #>  #> $tab_digits$col_pct #> [1] 0 #>  #> $tab_digits$total_pct #> [1] 0 #>  #>   # Get all options in the "tab_default" category tabstats_options("tab_default") #> $vb_char #> [1] "│" #>  #> $vb_top #> [1] "┬" #>  #> $vb_mid #> [1] "┼" #>  #> $vb_bottom #> [1] "┴" #>  #> $border_char #> [1] "─" #>  #> $header_underline #> [1] "─" #>  #> $truncate_message #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $nrows #> [1] 10 #>   # Get all options in the "tab_digits" category tabstats_options("tab_digits") #> $ex #> [1] 1 #>  #> $row_pct #> [1] 0 #>  #> $col_pct #> [1] 0 #>  #> $total_pct #> [1] 0 #>   # Get a specific option tabstats_options("tab_default", "vb_top") #> [1] "┬" tabstats_options("tab_digits", "ex") #> [1] 1  # Using backward compatibility (system will find the right category) tabstats_options("vb_top") #> [1] "┬" tabstats_options("ex") #> [1] 1  # Modify an option tabstats_options("tab_default", "border_char", "+") tabstats_options("tab_digits", "ex", 2)  # Using backward compatibility for modification tabstats_options("border_char", "+") tabstats_options("ex", 2)

title: Style specification for table_default() — td_style
text: Constructs validated style object use table_default().

title: Style specification for table_default() — td_style
code: td_style(...)

title: Style specification for table_default() — td_style
text: ... Named style entries. name must column name, column index string (e.g. "1"), "title". value string function \(ctx) ... ctx context list.

title: Style specification for table_default() — td_style
text: object class c("td_style", "tabstats_style").

title: Style specification for table_default() — td_style
code: td_style(mpg = "cyan", cyl = "magenta") #> <tabstats_style / td_style> #>   mpg: "cyan" #>   cyl: "magenta" td_style(mpg = \(ctx) cli::col_red(ctx$value), title = "bold") #> <tabstats_style / td_style> #>   mpg: <function> #>   title: "bold"

title: tabstats 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
